batches.title: Monday Lunch — Cumin-Paprika Chicken & Roasted Peppers for Mexican Bowl
steps: 1. Weigh 180g (Diego) + 140g (Diana) = 320g total chicken breast for Monday lunch. Slice into 1.5cm strips.
2. In a bowl combine 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp paprika, 0.5 tsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp lime juice. Toss chicken strips in marinade. Cover and refrigerate for Monday.
3. Slice 140g red bell pepper and 110g yellow bell pepper into strips for Monday's bowl.
4. Toss pepper strips with 1 tsp olive oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, pinch salt. Spread on a lined baking tray.
5. Roast peppers at 200°C for 20 min until slightly charred at the edges.
6. Allow peppers to cool completely, then store in an airtight container in the fridge.
7. Cook the marinated chicken Monday morning or store raw in the fridge (cook fresh Monday at lunch — see day_of_steps).
8. Drain and rinse 1 can of chickpeas (use 80g Diego + 60g Diana = 140g); store in a small airtight container.
yields: 320g marinated raw chicken strips (fridge, cook Monday)

day_of_assembly.Monday: Lunch — Mexican Chicken & Chickpea Bowl: grill marinated chicken strips in a hot pan 5–6 min; combine with roasted peppers, warmed chickpeas and freshly cooked brown rice; squeeze lime over bowl.

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Monday Dinner — Cooked Quinoa & Roasted Broccoli-Carrot for Salmon Bowl
steps: 1. Rinse 150g dry quinoa (total household, generous portion to cover Monday dinner + any carry-over).
2. Bring 300ml salted water to a boil; add quinoa, reduce to simmer, cover and cook 12–14 min until water is absorbed.
3. Fluff with fork, spread on a tray to cool, then store in an airtight container in the fridge.
4. Cut 200g broccoli into small florets and slice 150g carrot into 0.5cm rounds (total for both Monday dinner portions).
5. Toss broccoli and carrot with 1 tsp olive oil, 0.5 tsp turmeric, pinch black pepper and salt.
6. Spread on a lined baking tray; roast at 200°C for 20 min until tender and lightly golden.
7. Cool completely; store in an airtight container in the fridge.
yields: ~300g cooked quinoa (fridge, safe up to day 3 = Wednesday)
350g roasted broccoli and carrot (fridge, safe up to day 4 = Thursday)

day_of_assembly.Monday: Dinner — Baked Salmon with Roasted Broccoli, Carrot & Quinoa Tabbouleh: reheat 80g quinoa + 80g roasted veg (Diego) or 60g quinoa + 80g veg (Diana) in a pan 3 min; stir in balsamic vinegar and olive oil; top with freshly baked salmon.

storage.safety_note: Add a splash of water before reheating to restore moisture. Use within 3 days.
